subject: Sociology
title: Weber's Three Dimensions
description: A quick win: understand Weber's Three Dimensions.

hook.text: A respected pastor earns little money but commands a room. Weber said stratification isn't just about wealth.

buildup.text: Weber proposed three dimensions of stratification: class (wealth), status (prestige), and party (political power).

discovery.text: These dimensions overlap but don't always align. A professor has prestige but less wealth than a business owner.

twist.text: Marx reduced everything to economics. Weber showed that honor and political influence create separate hierarchies.

climax.text: Modern sociology uses all three dimensions to map inequality more accurately than any single measure allows.

punchline.text: Inequality isn't one ladder. It's three, and they don't line up.

quiz.question: What are Weber's three dimensions of stratification?
